summary: DHH announces that 37signals is switching its Ops and Ruby programming teams entirely to Omarchy, their custom Arch-derived Linux distribution, over the next three years. The move also means replacing MacBooks with Framework laptops and desktops. He argues that Linux offers superior control over your computing destiny compared to closed-source commercial operating systems. While acknowledging trade-offs like shorter battery life, he points to significant productivity gains from running Rails test suites much faster on Linux with native Docker support. He draws parallels to the early days of Ruby adoption, framing Omarchy as another contrarian bet that feels special before it goes mainstream.
key_points: 37signals will transition its Ops and Ruby teams from Macs to Omarchy (Arch Linux) and Framework hardware over three years
Nobody questions mandating Macs or Windows, but mandating Linux is treated as surprising — DHH sees this double standard as illogical
AMD processors and PC hardware have closed much of the gap with Apple Silicon, especially on performance if not yet on power efficiency
Rails test suites run almost twice as fast on a Framework Desktop with Linux compared to the fastest Mac, thanks to native Docker support
DHH compares the excitement of Omarchy to early Ruby and early OSX — a feeling of discovering something special before widespread adoption
money_quotes: It's funny how nobody bats an eye when the company mandate is to use Macs or Windows, but when the prescription is Linux, it's suddenly surprising.
Your ability to control your own destiny with Linux is far superior to what you'll get from a closed-source, commercial operating system. Of course it is! The code is literally all there!
I started using Ruby to build Basecamp when we could barely fill a room in American with professional Ruby programmers. This is what we do here!
A small concession on a rare occasion, but nothing like the performance AND battery deficit we willingly endured for decades on the Mac before Apple switched to their own chips.
Even the fastest Mac, an M4 Max, is almost twice as slow as a Framework Desktop machine running Linux, which can do Docker natively.
Omarchy is already by far-and-away my favorite computing environment. Right up there in joy and wonder with the old Amiga days or early OSX.
Very reminiscent of the excitement in the early Ruby days. Knowing you'd found something super special that wasn't yet widely distributed (but poised to be).
key_insight: DHH is betting that Linux on open hardware can replace Macs for professional Rails development, trading minor battery life concessions for vastly superior performance, openness, and control.
